item.title: Why do these physics tricks feel unusual?
item.description: A simple setup flips your expectation in a fun physics demo, showing how science can surprise us in a safe, educational way.

media:content.media:title: Why do these physics tricks feel unusual?
media:content.media:description: A simple setup flips your expectation in a fun physics demo, showing how science can surprise us in a safe, educational way.

item.description: A fly is placed in a vacuum chamber to illustrate how changing pressure affects the environment.

item.title: A drop of water becomes a lens
item.description: A simple water drop can act like a lens, bending light through refraction in a clear optics demonstration.

item.title: A volcano reaction you didn't do in school
item.description: I swear, a kid could invent a time machine and still be beat by a baking soda volcano at a science fair.
